label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2

extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won the sprint after VAN POPPEL Danny delivered a perfect leadout, beating PEDERSEN Mads on the line as MERLIER Tim and TEUNISSEN Mike lost critical positioning in the final roundabouts.
tactical_patterns: VAN POPPEL Danny's leadout execution proves BORA's sprint train remains functional without a marquee sprinter benefiting directly.
Multiple roundabouts within 2km unwound the sprint vortex, causing positioning chaos that eliminated MERLIER Tim's traditional speed advantage.
Break caught at 60km despite ALPECIN-DECEUNINCK doing heavy chase work for MERLIER Tim, revealing team misjudged energy allocation.
Time gaps declared in sprint finish allowed TEUNISSEN Mike to take red jersey, suggesting commissaires saw positioning disruption.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: demonstrated return to form by holding rhythm through technical roundabout chaos where MERLIER lost composure
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: lost critical positioning rubbing shoulders with MCLAY Daniel, suggesting poor technical handling under pressure
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: showed aggressive intent at intermediate sprint 17km out, maintaining form through long technical finale
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: provided textbook leadout despite no team sprinter benefiting, indicating high professional standards
rider_signals.VAN DEN BERG Julius: neutral: secured KOM jersey from breakaway on sole categorized climb, positioning for polka dot defense
